topic: A reviewable content factory built with ChatGPT Work and Scheduled Tasks
thesis: The useful automation is not unattended publishing; it is recurring research and production that pauses at human judgment points.

sources.notes: Verified against the official ChatGPT Learn page on 2026-07-15. A cropped browser screenshot is used as visible source evidence. Product availability may change.
Verified against the official Scheduled Tasks documentation on 2026-07-15. The documentation recommends testing the prompt manually before scheduling it.
Licensed stock footage selected through the Pexels page and license link on 2026-07-15. Used as illustrative B-roll, not factual evidence.
Licensed stock footage selected through the Pexels page and license link on 2026-07-15. Used as illustrative B-roll, not factual evidence.

facts.claim: ChatGPT Work can break a goal into steps and work for hours on a longer-running task.
facts.sensitivity: Do not imply that every task will run for hours or complete without review.

facts.claim: ChatGPT Work can gather context from files and plugins and take actions across a workflow.
facts.sensitivity: Available tools, permissions, and connectors determine which actions are possible.

facts.claim: ChatGPT Work is designed to create reviewable finished work such as documents, presentations, spreadsheets, and Sites.
A user can follow progress, change direction, and approve important actions while ChatGPT Work is running.
facts.sensitivity: Editorial approval gates in this video are an implementation choice built on this review model.

facts.claim: Scheduled Tasks can run once, on a recurring schedule, in response to an event, or as a monitor for changes.
facts.sensitivity: A schedule does not expand permissions or remove approval requirements.

facts.claim: Scheduled Tasks can be combined with skills, and their runs can be reviewed in the Scheduled inbox.
facts.sensitivity: The exact task UI and product availability may change.

facts.claim: OpenAI recommends testing a task prompt manually before putting it on a schedule.
facts.sensitivity: This supports the MVP-first recommendation; it is not a guarantee of later task quality.
